name: ratified decisions (ADR index)
description: AI features are typed agents in @resonance/ai via the AI-SDK-v6 Gateway + typed agent/tool registry; durable workflows deferred. Open ADR-0009 only when changing agent architecture.

name: structured runner = forced single tool call
description: runAgentStructured forces the tool call (toolChoice:'required') and stops after one step (stopWhen: stepCountIs(1)) so the tool executes once with no extra closing model turn; it reads result.toolResults.at(-1).output as the agent's Output. Throws AgentError when there are no tools, the required tool wasn't called, or generation failed. The tool handler is the source of Output (e.g. profile-gen's saveProfile returns {profileId}). Context-dependent tools use the factory seam defineProfileGenAgent({userId, currentRoles, db, embedder}) that closes over server state — the registry handler type stays (input)=>Promise<...>.

name: AI SDK v6 (6.0.x) API specifics
description: Pinned against ai@6.0.220: test utils are MockLanguageModelV3 / MockEmbeddingModelV3 + simulateReadableStream from 'ai/test' (V3, not V2/V4). Multi-step stop is stepCountIs (not isStepCount). Tools use tool({description, inputSchema: zodSchema, execute}). LanguageModelV3 finishReason is an OBJECT { unified: 'stop'|'tool-calls'|..., raw } (not a string). Usage is nested { inputTokens:{total,noCache,cacheRead,cacheWrite}, outputTokens:{total,text,reasoning} }. Model/EmbeddingModel accept a bare 'provider/model' string routed via the global Gateway provider. generateText result.toolResults[i] has .toolName/.input/.output. Confirm shapes from installed node_modules dts, not memory.

title: ProfileGen: generate draft, commit separately
rationale: The onboarding UX edits the draft before publishing, so generation must persist nothing and the role flip must happen post-approval; one exported commit fn keeps the ai->db ordering seam (ADR-0004) intact.

name: Live-by-default AI providers + test-only fakes (ADR-0018)
description: resolveModel/resolveEmbedder are live-by-default with key-precedence provider selection and fail-closed: resolveModel = opts.model (DI, wins so tests never hit live) -> AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY returns the provider/model string for the AI SDK global Gateway -> ANTHROPIC_API_KEY uses @ai-sdk/anthropic direct (strip the 'anthropic/' prefix; anthropic/* ids only) -> throw AgentError. resolveEmbedder = AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY (voyage/voyage-3.5 string) -> VOYAGE_API_KEY (voyage-ai-provider textEmbeddingModel) -> throw; keeps the 1024-dim contract. NO RESONANCE_FAKES runtime branch. createFakeModel + createFakeEmbedder moved to the test-only @resonance/ai/test subpath (src/test/index.ts; package.json exports './test', mirroring @resonance/db/testing) and injected via RunInput.model / the Embedder DI seam — never imported by shipped code, not on the main entrypoint. Dep pinning matters: @ai-sdk/anthropic must be ^3 (provider spec v3, matches ai@6.0.220); the default ^4 pulls @ai-sdk/provider v4 and mismatches. voyage-ai-provider ^4 targets provider ^3 (ok). Tests stub env via vi.stubEnv to assert selection + fail-closed deterministically. Mirrors the auth resolveMail per-seam key-precedence pattern (mx-23d37c).

name: shared-provider-selection-and-fail-fast-config
description: resolveModel + resolveEmbedder share ONE internal selectProvider ladder (provider-config.ts): Gateway (AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY) → direct provider key → fail-closed AgentError, with lazily-built (thunk) branch results so only the chosen branch constructs a client and the direct builder can itself fail closed (e.g. anthropic/*-only guard). Keeps key precedence in one home so the two seams can't drift; selectProvider is internal (NOT on @resonance/ai entrypoint). Paired with exported assertAiConfigured() — a fail-fast boot-time gate that checks env PRESENCE ONLY (no live call) that model AND embedding are JOINTLY resolvable (AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY, or both ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and VOYAGE_API_KEY), mirroring scripts/verify-live.mjs's joint gate; a partial config (ANTHROPIC without VOYAGE) now fails up front naming exactly what is missing instead of passing resolveModel then throwing in resolveEmbedder at profile-commit. Call assertAiConfigured at onboarding entry points before interview/generation.
